game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **The Game of Life:** Players engage in a digital board game experience, taking turns to spin a numerical spinner (1-10 spaces) by tapping it, which dictates their movement along a linear path on a 3D board. Landing on specific spaces prompts players to make life choices (e.g., "Go to College" or "Start Career"), encounter events, or participate in simple tap-based minigames where success grants bonus in-game currency. The core loop involves accumulating wealth and "Life Points" through strategic choices and successful minigames, with progression defined by advancing through life stages towards the "Retirement" space. The game concludes when all players reach retirement, or a player goes bankrupt and is eliminated, with the winner being the player who amassed the most total wealth (money plus assets). Multiplayer modes support up to three local players on one device or online matchmaking against other players, with a "Fast Mode" offering alternative win conditions like being the first to reach $1,000,000.
game_info.description: Experience a streamlined single-player journey through life's key decisions! Spin the spinner to move along a winding path, making strategic choices at career, education, and investment spaces. Land on event spaces to trigger quick tap-based minigames where success earns bonus cash. Accumulate wealth through smart decisions and skillful play. Reach the Retirement space with the highest net worth to win, but watch out—poor choices can lead to bankruptcy and game over!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (↑/↓ or ←/→): Navigate menu choices at decision spaces
Space: Spin the spinner to move forward / Confirm selection / Tap during minigames
Z: Quick action for minigame interactions
Enter: Start the game from the title screen
Esc: Pause/unpause the game
R: Return to start screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic game mechanics: spinner functionality, movement along the path, and reaching spaces. This validates that the core game loop works correctly with automated inputs simulating player actions.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically press Space to spin and move forward repeatedly. Make random but valid choices at decision spaces. Participate in minigames with timed inputs to accumulate some wealth.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player successfully moves through multiple spaces, makes choices, completes minigames, and either reaches retirement or continues playing. No crashes or stuck states occur during automated play.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing the optimal win strategy by making the best financial decisions and maximizing minigame performance. This validates that skilled play leads to victory with high net worth.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Choose high-reward career paths and investments at decision spaces. Execute near-perfect timing on all minigames to maximize bonus earnings. Optimize every decision for wealth accumulation.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player reaches retirement with substantial wealth (ideally $500,000+), triggering GAME_OVER_WIN state. The test demonstrates that optimal play results in a satisfying victory condition.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing poor decision-making and bankruptcy mechanics. This validates that the game can end in failure when the player makes consistently bad choices or performs poorly in minigames.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately choose low-reward options at decision spaces. Perform poorly in minigames by missing timing windows. Make risky investments that may fail.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The player either reaches retirement with low wealth or potentially goes bankrupt if funds drop below zero, triggering GAME_OVER_LOSE. This confirms the challenge and consequence systems work correctly.
